title: Trump Restructures Section 232 Steel, Aluminum, and Copper Tariffs
detail: Trump signed a proclamation under Section 232 restructuring tariffs on steel, aluminum, copper, and derivative products. Articles made almost entirely of those metals now face a flat 50% tariff on full customs value, up from a narrower base calculation. The new rates take effect at 12:01 a.m. ET on April 6, 2026, aligning with the one-year anniversary of 'Liberation Day' tariffs. Trading partners warned of retaliatory measures.

media.caption: Trump restructures Section 232 tariffs on steel, aluminum, and copper

title: Trump Imposes Up to 100% Tariff on Patented Pharmaceutical Imports
detail: Trump signed an executive order under Section 232 imposing up to 100% tariffs on patented pharmaceutical products and active pharmaceutical ingredients, aimed at forcing onshoring of drug manufacturing. Exemptions apply to generics, biosimilars, and orphan drugs. The pharmaceutical industry warned of significant price increases and threats to US investment. The order accelerates pressure on major drugmakers to relocate manufacturing to the United States.

media.caption: Trump unveils 100% tariff on patented pharmaceutical imports

title: ICE Arrests Jordanian National in Milwaukee on Terror Financing Charges
detail: DHS announced that ICE, working with the U.S. Marshals Service, arrested Salah Salem Sarsour, a Jordanian national, in Milwaukee, Wisconsin on charges of funding terror organizations and lying on immigration forms. The arrest was part of continued interior enforcement operations targeting individuals with alleged national security violations.
